stefan_boltzmann_scalar.M2_meaning: M2: pi^4 from Riemann zeta(4) = pi^4/90

stefan_boltzmann_dirac.M2_meaning: M2: pi^4 from Riemann zeta(4) = pi^4/90

rows.tag: M1: Hopf-base measure factor 1/(2 pi^2) from Vol(S^2)/(2pi)^3
M2: pi^4 from Riemann zeta(4) = pi^4/90
M1 x M2 product gives net pi^2/90
M2: pi^2 in Jacobi theta_2 modular transformation
M2: sqrt(pi) from Seeley-DeWitt a_0 on unit S^3
M1: sqrt(pi) from inverse theta on the imaginary-time circle

rows.quantity: Matsubara modes 2 pi k / beta (bosonic) or 2 pi (k+1/2) / beta (fermionic)
rows.tag: M1: 2 pi from imaginary-time circle circumference (S^1_beta factor)
M2 collapses to rational at half-integer Hurwitz shift (Paper 35 KG-3)
M2 collapses to rational at half-integer Hurwitz shift (Paper 35 KG-5)
M2: rational eta(4)/zeta(4) = 7/8 from boundary condition

summary.paper_35_prediction_1_test: Every pi-source is associated with a continuous integration over a temporal/spectral parameter promoted from the discrete spectrum: (a) Matsubara modes 2 pi k / beta come from compactification of     imaginary time (Paper 35 KG-2 mechanism). (b) The 1/(2 pi^2) Hopf measure factor enters from the Weyl     asymptotic d^3k/(2pi)^3, i.e. from the *continuum* limit of     the spatial integration. (c) The sqrt(pi) Seeley-DeWitt coefficients come from the     Mellin transform on s of the heat-kernel trace. All of these are continuous-integration projections. The rational values (Casimir 1/240, -17/480; eta 7/8) come from DISCRETE Hurwitz / Bernoulli evaluations and contain no pi — consistent with Paper 35 Prediction 1 verbatim.

headline_findings: Stefan-Boltzmann scalar -pi^2/90 reproduced as exact M1 (1/(2pi^2)) x IBP (1/3) x M2 (pi^4/15) factorisation; sympy residual 0.
Stefan-Boltzmann Dirac per Weyl -7pi^2/720 reproduced as exact M1 x IBP x M2 (eta(4) = (7/8) zeta(4)) factorisation; sympy residual 0.
Scalar S^3 Casimir = 1/240 (RATIONAL, no pi). Dirac S^3 Casimir = -17/480 (RATIONAL, no pi). Both Paper 35 KG-3 / KG-5 verbatim.
Modular residual epsilon(t) = sum (-1)^m sqrt(pi) e^{-m^2 pi^2 / t} [t^{-3/2} - 2 m^2 pi^2 t^{-5/2} - (1/2) t^{-1/2}] from Sprint MR-B; modular exponent pi^2; ring sqrt(pi) Q + pi^2 Q (M2).
Tensor factorisation Tr e^{-s D_total^2} = K_spatial(s) * K_temporal(s); leading temporal kernel beta/(2 sqrt(pi s)) from Jacobi theta inversion (M1).
10 transcendental sources audited; M1 count 3, M2 count 4, M3 count 0, product M1xM2 = 1, rational collapses = 2.
Paper 35 Prediction 1 verbatim on the tensor product: every pi has a continuous-integration source (Matsubara compactification, Hopf measure, Seeley-DeWitt Mellin, theta inversion).
